2603.26449.title: ClimateCheck 2026: Scientific Fact-Checking and Disinformation Narrative Classification of Climate-related Claims
2603.26449.paper_summary: Automatically verifying climate-related claims against scientific literature is a challenging task, complicated by the specialised nature of scholarly evidence and the diversity of rhetorical strategies underlying climate disinformation. ClimateCheck 2026 is the second iteration of a shared task addressing this challenge, expanding on the 2025 edition with tripled training data and a new disinformation narrative classification task. Running from January to February 2026 on the CodaBench platform, the competition attracted 20 registered participants and 8 leaderboard submissions, with systems combining dense retrieval pipelines, cross-encoder ensembles, and large language models with structured hierarchical reasoning. In addition to standard evaluation metrics (Recall@K and Binary Preference), we adapt an automated framework to assess retrieval quality under incomplete annotations, exposing systematic biases in how conventional metrics rank systems. A cross-task analysis further reveals that not all climate disinformation is equally verifiable, potentially implicating how future fact-checking systems should be designed.
2603.26449.paper_summary_zh: 自動驗證與氣候相關的主張與科學文獻的符合性是一項具挑戰性的任務，因為學術證據的專業性質以及氣候錯誤資訊背後的修辭策略的多樣性使其變得更加複雜。ClimateCheck 2026 是針對這一挑戰的共享任務的第二次迭代，基於 2025 年版本擴展了三倍的訓練數據並引入了一個新的錯誤資訊敘事分類任務。比賽於 2026 年 1 月至 2 月在 CodaBench 平台上進行，吸引了 20 名註冊參賽者和 8 次排行榜提交，系統結合了密集檢索管道、交叉編碼器集成和大型語言模型與結構化層次推理。此外，除了標準評估指標（Recall@K 和 Binary Preference）外，我們還調整了一個自動化框架，以評估在不完整註釋下的檢索質量，揭示了傳統指標在排名系統時的系統性偏見。跨任務分析進一步顯示，並非所有氣候錯誤資訊都同樣可驗證，這可能影響未來事實查核系統的設計方式。
2603.26449.author: Raia Abu Ahmad et.al.
2603.26449.authors: Raia Abu Ahmad, Max Upravitelev, Aida Usmanova, Veronika Solopova, Georg Rehm
